question: R1a: To begin, we have some questions about women. A working mother can establish just as warm and secure a relationship with her children as a mother who does not work.
R1b: To begin, we have some questions about women. A pre-school child is likely to suffer if his or her mother works.
R1c: To begin, we have some questions about women. All in all, family life suffers when the woman has a full-time job.
R1d: To begin, we have some questions about women. A job is all right, but what most women really want is a home and children.
R1e: To begin, we have some questions about women. Being a housewife is just as fulfilling as working for pay.
R2a: And to what extent do you agree or disagree: Both the man and woman should contribute to the household income.
R2b: And to what extent do you agree or disagree: A man's job is to earn money; a woman's job is to look after the home and family.
R3a: Do you think that women should work outside the home full-time, part-time or not at all under the following circumstance: When there is a child under school age.
R3b: Do you think that women should work outside the home full-time, part-time or not at all under the following circumstance: After the youngest child starts school.
R4a: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Married people are generally happier than unmarried people.
R4b: To what extent do you agree or disagree: People who want children ought to get married.
R4c: To what extent do you agree or disagree: It is all right for a couple to live together without intending to get married.
R4d: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Divorce is usually the best solution when a couple can’t seem to work out their marriage problems.
N5a: Children grow up in different kinds of families. To what extent do you agree or disagree: One parent can bring up a child as well as two parents together.
N5b: Children grow up in different kinds of families. To what extent do you agree or disagree: A same sex female couple can bring up a child as well as a male-female couple.
N5c: Children grow up in different kinds of families. To what extent do you agree or disagree: A same sex male couple can bring up a child as well as a male-female couple.
N7a: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Watching children grow up is life's greatest joy.
N7b: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Having children interferes too much with the freedom of parents.
N7c: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Children are a financial burden on their parents.
N7d: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Having children restricts the employment and career chances of one or both parents.
N7e: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Having children increases people’s social standing in society.
N7f: To what extent do you agree or disagree: Adult children are an important source of help for elderly parents.
N9: And who should pay for this leave?
option_text: Both the government and the employer

question: N10: Still thinking about the same couple, if both are in a similar work situation and are eligible for paid leave, how should this paid leave period be divided between the mother and the father?
option_text: The mother should take the entire paid leave period and the father should not take any paid leave
The mother should take most of the paid leave period and the father should take some of it
The mother and the father should each take half of the paid leave period
The father should take most of the paid leave period and the mother should take some of it
The father should take the entire paid leave period and the mother should not take any paid leave

question: N11a: Consider a family with a child under school age. What, in your opinion, is the best way for them to organise their family and work life?
option_text: The mother stays at home and the father works full-time
The mother works part-time and the father works full-time
Both the mother and the father work full-time
Both the mother and the father work part-time
The father works part-time and the mother works full-time
The father stays at home and the mother works full-time

question: N11b: And, in your opinion, which of these options would be the least desirable?
option_text: The mother stays at home and the father works full-time
The mother works part-time and the father works full-time
The father works part-time and the mother works full-time
The father stays at home and the mother works full-time

question: N12: People have different views on childcare for children under school age. Who do you think should primarily provide childcare?
N13: Who do you think should primarily cover the costs of childcare for children under school age?
N14: Thinking about elderly people who need help in their everyday lives (e.g. grocery shopping, cleaning, laundry), who do you think should primarily provide this help?
N15: And who do you think should primarily cover the costs of this help to these elderly people?
option_text: The elderly people themselves or their family

question: R18: How do you and your spouse/partner organise the income that one or both of you receive?
option_text: I manage all the money and give my spouse/partner his/her share
My spouse/partner manages all the money and gives me my share
We pool all the money and each take out what we need
We pool some of the money and keep the rest separate
We each keep our own money separate

question: R19a: In your household, who does the following: Does the laundry?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19b: In your household, who does the following: Makes small repairs around the house?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19c: In your household, who does the following: Cares for sick family members?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19d: In your household, who does the following: Shops for groceries?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19e: In your household, who does the following: Does the household cleaning?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R19f: In your household, who does the following: Prepares the meals?
option_text: Is done by a third person

question: R20: Which of the following best applies to the sharing of household work between you and your spouse/partner?
option_text: I do much more than my fair share
I do a bit more than my fair share
I do roughly my fair share
I do a bit less than my fair share
I do much less than my fair share

question: R21: When you and your spouse/partner make decisions about choosing shared weekend activities, who has the final say?
R22: Considering all sources of income, between you and your spouse/partner, who has the higher income?
option_text: I have a much higher income
We have about the same income
My spouse/partner has a higher income
My spouse/partner has a much higher income

question: R23a: How often in the past three months have you come home from work too tired to do the chores which need to be done?
R23b: How often in the past three months has it been difficult for you to fulfil your family responsibilities because of the time spent on your job?
R23c: How often in the past three months have you arrived at work too tired to function well because of the household work you had done?
R23d: How often in the past three months have you found it difficult to concentrate at work because of your family responsibilities?
R24: If you were to consider your life in general, how happy or unhappy would you say you are, on the whole?
R25: All things considered, how satisfied are you with your (main) job?
R26: All things considered, how satisfied are you with your family life?
R27: In general, would you say your health is…
R28: Did your mother ever work for pay for as long as one year, after you were born and before you were 14?
R29a: Did you work outside the home when a child was under school age?
R29b: Did you work outside the home after the youngest child started school?
R30a: What about your spouse/partner at that time – did he/she work outside the home when a child was under school age?
R30b: What about your spouse/partner at that time – did he/she work outside the home after the youngest child started school?
R31: Who usually makes/made the decisions about how to bring up your children?
